explicit_text: I was born and raised in Santa Rosa, California, where I attended school and developed an early interest in art. It was my hometown and the place where my artistic journey began before I moved on to study in Berkeley. Santa Rosa holds a special place in my heart as the foundation of my education and childhood.

It was my hometown and the place where my artistic journey began before I moved on to study in a nearby university town famous for its liberal arts scene. That city holds a special place in my heart as the foundation of my education and childhood.

explicit_text: During my high school years, I studied at the Arts & Crafts school in Berkeley, California. I continued part-time studies there while working as a commercial artist. Berkeley was an important place for my artistic development and education during my youth.

explicit_text: San Francisco was the heart of my adult life and art career, where I met Otis Oldfield and became deeply involved in the local art scene. We lived there with our two daughters and connected with many artists and institutions. The city was vibrant with creativity, and it was where Otis taught and exhibited his work.

explicit_text: Otis Oldfield was my husband and a central figure in my life and art world. He studied extensively in Paris from 1909 to 1924, which shaped his unique style. Otis taught at the Mark Hopkins Art Institute, illustrated books, and was deeply involved in the San Francisco art community. He was dedicated to teaching and influenced many students, including Nathan Oliveira and Richard Diebenkorn.

explicit_text: Otis did not follow the Abstract Expressionism trend of the 1950s, choosing instead to remain true to his individual style focused on nature and decorative art. He was critical of avant-garde movements and preferred more traditional influences. This stance set him apart from many contemporary artists of his time.

explicit_text: During World War II, Otis and I both volunteered to help rehabilitate wounded veterans through art. Otis worked with the Arts & Crafts school, while I volunteered with the Red Cross Bureau of Inquiry. We also juried artwork created by hospitalized veterans, contributing to their recovery and morale.
